title: Solana's Token Supply Dynamics Shift with Protocol Changes
summary: Solana currently issues about 60,000 new tokens daily but burns only 650. Three proposed changes could reverse this imbalance, impacting token scarcity and price. The real story is in the protocol-level adjustments that are starting to take effect.
body:
Solana's token supply dynamics are undergoing significant changes. Currently, the network issues approximately 60,000 new SOL tokens each day, while only burning around 650. This imbalance has raised concerns about inflation and token scarcity.

However, three proposed changes are set to flip this math. These changes aim to increase the burn rate and reduce the issuance rate, potentially making SOL more scarce. The price of SOL has started to rebound, but the real story lies in these protocol-level adjustments that are about to take effect.

For everyday users, this means that the value of SOL could become more stable or even appreciate if the proposed changes successfully reduce the supply of new tokens. It also highlights the importance of protocol-level decisions in shaping the future of cryptocurrencies.

To stay informed, keep an eye on the implementation of these proposed changes. The timeline and specifics of these changes will be crucial in determining the future trajectory of SOL's price and supply dynamics. Read more → ARTICLE_URL
twitterThread: Solana issues 60,000 new tokens daily but burns just 650. Three proposed changes could reverse this imbalance. 🤯
The real story is at the protocol level. New supply is entering circulation, but adjustments aim to increase burns and reduce issuance.
Keep an eye on these changes—they could stabilize or even boost SOL's price. Read more → https://cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-07-04-solanas-token-supply-dynamics-shift-with-protocol-changes
